criteria_notes.Developer Experience: Strong primitives, but more workflow design overhead than lighter SDKs.
criteria_notes.Agent Capabilities: Best-in-class branching, checkpoints, review loops, and multi-step control.
criteria_notes.Context & Memory: Shared state plus checkpoints make long-running workflows easier to reason about.
criteria_notes.Deployment & Hosting: Flexible Python deployment story, but more infra decisions fall on the team.
criteria_notes.Security & Compliance: Strong fit for auditable flows, but enterprise controls come from your hosting choices.

memory.model: Durable shared workflow state with checkpoint-and-resume
risks: State schema drift if not maintained carefully

deployment.fit: Strong for Python backends, background workers, and durable orchestration services.
deployment.hosting: Works well in containers, long-running services, and queue-backed agent systems.
deployment.tradeoff: You get control, but you own more runtime and persistence decisions.

security.fit: Good when you need explicit reviewer gates and auditable state transitions.
security.tradeoff: Compliance posture depends on the surrounding stack rather than the framework alone.

criteria_notes.Developer Experience: Fastest path to specialist agents, tools, and traces in one SDK.
criteria_notes.Agent Capabilities: Great handoffs and tool use, weaker for highly complex orchestration graphs.
criteria_notes.Context & Memory: Run context works well, but not as durable or explicit as checkpointed workflow state.
criteria_notes.Deployment & Hosting: Very straightforward for app teams shipping Python services quickly.
criteria_notes.Security & Compliance: Good defaults and guardrails, but less enterprise-governance depth than heavier systems.

risks: Less durable than checkpointed workflow engines

deployment.fit: Excellent for API apps and product teams that want to ship quickly.
deployment.hosting: Works cleanly in Python services and common container/serverless setups.
deployment.tradeoff: Simple to host, but complex orchestration eventually needs additional structure.

security.fit: Solid for app-level guardrails and bounded specialist flows.
security.tradeoff: Enterprise review and compliance workflows need extra implementation.

criteria_notes.Developer Experience: Flexible but conversation-heavy; easier to drift without strong constraints.
criteria_notes.Agent Capabilities: Strong for debate, role interaction, and exploratory specialist teams.
criteria_notes.Context & Memory: Transcript-driven memory is intuitive but easy to bury or distort over time.
criteria_notes.Deployment & Hosting: Deployable as Python services, but runtime behavior needs tighter production controls.
criteria_notes.Security & Compliance: Natural for exploration, less natural for strict enterprise governance.

deployment.fit: Works best when experimentation and analyst-style collaboration matter more than strict determinism.
deployment.hosting: Deployable in Python services, but production hardening matters.
deployment.tradeoff: Conversation flexibility can become runtime unpredictability.

security.fit: Lower fit for heavily governed production paths.
controls: Human review can be layered in
security.tradeoff: You need to add stronger stop rules, logging, and policy controls yourself.

criteria_notes.Developer Experience: Readable manager-task structure makes onboarding easier.
criteria_notes.Agent Capabilities: Good for role-based workflows, weaker for graph-like control and deep memory models.
criteria_notes.Context & Memory: Task outputs are understandable, but manager synthesis can compress nuance.
criteria_notes.Deployment & Hosting: Straightforward Python deployment and easy to explain to application teams.
criteria_notes.Security & Compliance: Reasonable with human checkpoints, but not inherently enterprise-governed.

risks: Loss of clause-level nuance in summaries

deployment.fit: Strong when teams want business-process framing without deep workflow-engine complexity.
deployment.hosting: Good Python service fit and practical for app teams.
deployment.tradeoff: More complex multi-agent state still needs additional design.

security.fit: Decent for product workflows with human review points.
security.tradeoff: Compliance and governance controls are mostly external to the framework.

criteria_notes.Developer Experience: Powerful, but heavier and broader than a focused application team may want.
criteria_notes.Agent Capabilities: Good governed workflows, plugin-based composition, and enterprise framing.
criteria_notes.Context & Memory: Shared memory and governed state models are stronger than lighter SDKs.
criteria_notes.Deployment & Hosting: Good fit for enterprise-controlled hosting environments.
criteria_notes.Security & Compliance: Best fit in the set for explicit governance, controls, and enterprise expectations.

memory.model: Governed shared state plus plugin/context memory

deployment.fit: Strong fit for controlled enterprise stacks and platform teams.
deployment.hosting: Works well in managed enterprise infrastructure and internal platforms.
deployment.tradeoff: Can feel heavy if the use case is small or fast-moving.

security.fit: Best enterprise-governance fit among the current comparison set.
security.tradeoff: Operational weight is the price of stronger governance.

criteria_notes.Developer Experience: Good when you think in events and retrieval-heavy pipelines, but not as immediate as simpler SDKs.
criteria_notes.Agent Capabilities: Strong evidence pipelines, event processing, and structured workflow steps.
criteria_notes.Context & Memory: Event payloads and workflow state are strong for retrieval-backed systems.
criteria_notes.Deployment & Hosting: Practical Python hosting story for service-oriented systems.
criteria_notes.Security & Compliance: Reasonable, but governance needs to come from the surrounding application stack.

risks: Implicit state loss if payloads get too thin

deployment.fit: Strong for retrieval-heavy agent systems and structured evidence workflows.
deployment.hosting: Good fit for Python services, async workers, and pipeline-oriented apps.
deployment.tradeoff: More workflow thinking required than handoff-style SDKs.

security.fit: Solid app-level fit but not inherently enterprise-governed.
security.tradeoff: Security posture depends on application implementation and hosting.

criteria_notes.Developer Experience: Strong TypeScript ergonomics for product teams.
criteria_notes.Agent Capabilities: Good mix of workflows, agents, and app-facing orchestration.
criteria_notes.Context & Memory: Useful workflow context, but less explicit/durable than checkpoint-heavy systems.
criteria_notes.Deployment & Hosting: Very attractive for TypeScript app stacks and product-facing runtimes.
criteria_notes.Security & Compliance: Fine for product teams, but governance posture depends on implementation.

deployment.fit: Best TypeScript-native fit in the set for product-oriented teams.
deployment.hosting: Strong fit for Node/container environments and product backend stacks.
deployment.tradeoff: Governance discipline is more app-owned than framework-owned.

security.fit: Good app-team fit, moderate enterprise-governance fit.
security.tradeoff: You still need to design stronger policy boundaries yourself.

criteria_notes.Developer Experience: Excellent when typed outputs and Python ergonomics matter.
criteria_notes.Agent Capabilities: Less opinionated on orchestration, stronger on correctness and validation.
criteria_notes.Context & Memory: Typed models preserve key information well, even without a heavy workflow engine.
criteria_notes.Deployment & Hosting: Straightforward Python deployment with strong testing ergonomics.
criteria_notes.Security & Compliance: Better than average thanks to schema validation and explicit output guarantees.

memory.model: Typed result models plus validated execution state
risks: You still design the broader memory/orchestration model

deployment.fit: Strong for Python teams prioritizing correctness and reliable structured outputs.
deployment.hosting: Easy service deployment and excellent local/test environments.
deployment.tradeoff: Less built-in orchestration guidance for large multi-agent systems.

security.fit: Strong for reliability-first and validation-heavy use cases.
security.tradeoff: Enterprise governance still requires additional surrounding infrastructure.
